goal: Add the downloaded mecha spider as a fourth selectable playable unit in the lobby, with its own stats, weapon tuning, and visible in-match model.
current_evidence: Project already has VehicleDefinition, VehicleStats, and WeaponStats resources.
Lobby vehicle choices are currently hardcoded in res://scenes/ui/lobby_start_screen.gd.
TankGame already resolves selected vehicle ids against exported vehicle_definitions.
Downloaded asset folder contains SK_ISO_Mech.fbx, seven animation FBXs, and five PBR textures under C:/Users/nkatz/Downloads/Mech/.
non_goals: Dedicated spider weapon model or custom aiming rig
Networking changes beyond existing match setup vehicle selection
Balancing beyond first playable prototype values
constraints: Prefer node/scene/Inspector resources over hardcoded behavior.
Do not touch unrelated dirty files unless the vehicle integration requires the same file.

Do not stage existing unrelated dirty work: TANK_MP_RUNBOOK.md, TANK_MP_VFX_ABC_PLAN.md, scenes/vfx/, scenes/player_character/dependencies/robot_model/3DGodotRobot.glb.import, or unrelated VFX changes.
If tank_controller.gd must be edited, preserve existing uncommitted VFX additions and stage only the combined in-scope file if tests pass.

planned_unit.design_intent: Slower top speed than light tank, better slope handling than tracked tanks, medium health, fast turret response, lower ammo but quick precision shots.

slices.acceptance: Roadmap records asset facts, scope, risks, slices, verification, and stop rules.
Return concise recommendations for lobby naming, stat language, and manual test wording; no file edits.
slices.notes: Recommended Spider Mech label, slope-ready walker description, precision skirmisher/hillfighter role, and manual gate wording.

slices.acceptance: Return likely import/scale/material/animation risks for the Mech FBX and what the smoke test should assert.
slices.notes: Identified visual_scene not yet applied, lobby hardcoded options, TankGame definition list, FBX scale/orientation/material risks, and recommended wrapper scene plus smoke assertions.

slices.acceptance: Asset files are copied into the project, imported by Godot, and available as res:// paths.
Spider Mech has definition, stats, weapon stats, and visual transform tuning in resources/scene.
Lobby exposes Spider Mech, TankGame resolves it, tank applies its stats/weapon/visual, and smoke proves selection can start.
slices.notes: Godot smoke passed with spider_mech selected: 4 ammo, 95 HP, visual override mesh count > 0, fire/reload/crater/capture still pass.

verification: Focused import probe confirms the spider visual scene instantiates and contains visible MeshInstance3D descendants.
Git staged-files check confirms unrelated dirty files are not staged.
known_follow_ups: FBX import reports missing internal SK_Mech_LOD*.fbm/Bake_* texture paths. The playable wrapper uses an explicit Godot material from the provided PBR textures, so this is deferred unless raw FBX material fidelity becomes a goal.
Separate animation FBXs are imported but not wired into gameplay. Idle/walk animation blending is deferred.
Spider Mech uses the existing tank controller/turret/muzzle path for v0.1 playability.

go_language.go: Spider Mech appears as a lobby choice, match starts with spider_mech setup, stats/weapon values apply, and the visual scene instantiates without runtime errors.
go_language.partial: Spider Mech is selectable and playable but material scale or animation polish needs a follow-up.
go_language.no_go: Mech asset cannot import, match startup breaks, or selection corrupts existing tank choices.
go_language.deferred: Walk/idle/death animation blending, custom spider movement physics, and dedicated spider weapon hardpoint.

stop_rules: Stop on secrets or private credentials.
Stop if imported asset licensing metadata forbids use.
Stop if Godot import/runtime errors cannot be fixed within this slice.
Stop rather than overwrite unrelated dirty work.
